text.title: CoT Basics Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for basic chain-of-thought prompting.

rule.statement: Reasoning goes inside `...` and the final answer inside `...`. Untagged free-text reasoning is unparseable.
rule.rationale: Without structural tags the runtime cannot reliably separate scratch-work from result; downstream consumers re-parse with brittle regex.

rule.statement: Caller parses `` content with a single regex and treats anything outside as opaque. Reasoning is logged but never displayed to end-users by default.
rule.rationale: Reasoning leaks confuse users and expose internal logic; logging keeps the audit trail without UX leak.

rule.statement: Output token budget for CoT calls is at least 2x non-CoT baseline. If hitting max_tokens, CoT was truncated and answer may be missing.
rule.rationale: CoT produces extra tokens; an undersized budget cuts the answer block.

rule.statement: When the model is a reasoning model (Claude Extended Thinking, o-series), skip prompt-side CoT — CoT happens internally and the explicit prompt adds cost without lift.
rule.rationale: Internal reasoning already produces the chain; the explicit prompt is redundant and inflates output tokens.

rule.statement: Measure CoT lift against the same task without CoT on a 50-case eval before keeping CoT in production. No measured lift → revert.
rule.rationale: CoT is often assumed-good but lift varies by task class; measurement keeps prompts honest.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire CoT prompt + parser + eval.
Single-step task or reasoning model in use → skip; CoT adds cost without lift.
